rulings.Case Title: ROBERT CULVER et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 24, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Honeywell International, Inc.¿s motion for summary adjudication: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:45a.m. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ELAINE PERCY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 24, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 9. Defendants The Regents of the University of California¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. This Court finds that defendant satisfied its burden of production, and plaintiffs have not produced any admissible evidence that defendant knew, or reasonably should have known, that installation, removal, disturbance and/or clean-up of asbestos-containing material could create a safety hazard of secondary exposure for a member of a household of a non-employee. Even if Ms. Percy¿s exposure to asbestos attributable to defendant was foreseeable, pursuant to Rowland v. Christian (1968) 69 Cal.2d 108 and Campbell v. Ford Motor Company (2012) 206 Cal.App.4th 15, public policy dictates against the creation of a duty in the present matter. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: LOUIS TROST VS. SEQUOIA VENTURES INC.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 24, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Albay Construction Company¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Specifically, plaintiff¿s deposition testimony demonstrates that he knows that the products/materials to which he was exposed by defendant contained asbestos. Further, in response to a direct question by defendant¿s counsel, plaintiff identified specific persons with knowledge of defendant¿s work in plaintiff¿s presence, and defendant failed to demonstrate that such individuals do not have knowledge to support plaintiff¿s claims against defendant. (See Trost depo., Vol. 5, 755:3-14.) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: BILLY JAMES SMITH VS. CROWN CORK & SEAL COMPANY, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 24, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Fraser¿s Boiler Service, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that that the sophisticated user defense applies to bar plaintiffs¿ claims against it. Specifically, Mr. Coker¿s deposition testimony precludes defendant from demonstrating that any alleged exposure to asbestos attributable to defendant occurred after the time plaintiff learned of the hazards of asbestos. Even if defendant had sustained its burden, Mr. Coker¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether any alleged exposure to asbestos attributable to defendant occurred before the time plaintiff learned of the hazards of asbestos. Finally, even if there was no evidence that any alleged exposure to asbestos attributable to defendant occurred before the time plaintiff learned of the hazards of asbestos, the sophisticated user defense would not be a complete bar to plaintiff¿s claims against defendant. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID WEDDING et al VS. 3M COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 24, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 7. Defendant Crane Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Wedding was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Even if plaintiffs had created a triable issue on that topic, plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether such exposure was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to Mr. Wedding¿s asbestos-related illness. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
